questions.objective_ref: Create a set from literals and from an iterable, and explain the uniqueness guarantee that removes duplicates automatically.
Create a set from literals and from an iterable, and explain the uniqueness guarantee (empty-set rule: {} is a dict, use set()).
Choose a set over a list when the task is about uniqueness, membership testing, or overlap between collections.
Apply the four core set operations (intersection) using operator and method forms.
Apply the four core set operations (symmetric difference) using operator and method forms.
Apply the four core set operations using operator (set-only) and method (any iterable) forms.
Use the in-place variants of the four operations to mutate a set instead of building a new one.
Compare two sets with subset, superset, and disjoint relations (subset/superset direction, proper vs non-proper).
Compare two sets with subset, superset, and disjoint relations (disjoint has no operator form).
Mutate a set with add, remove, and discard, and describe how remove and discard differ on a missing element.
Explain why set membership is roughly constant time (O(1)) via hashing compared with scanning a list (O(n)).
Use frozenset when you need an immutable set (immutability makes it hashable, so it can be an element of another set).
Write a simple set comprehension by analogy with the list comprehension you already know.
Create a set from literals and from an iterable (empty-set rule: call set() with no arguments).
Mutate a set with add, remove, and discard (discard deletes without raising on a missing element).
